title: Weapons
text: | For Nightreign , seek Nightreign Weapons . Weapons , also known as Armaments , are equipment items in Elden Ring and Shadow of the Erdtree . Weapons scale in power based on the player's attributes . Several weapons can be wielded two-handed, affecting their attack strength and animation patterns. For keyboard controls, the command to switch to two-handed mode is to hold E and then click the attack button of the weapon in question. There are two types of Armaments: generic weapons that are upgraded with Smithing Stones and unique weapons, which are bolstered with Somber Smithing Stones . Armaments may be improved by interacting with Smithing Master Hewg , Smithing Master Iji , or the Smithing Table at the Church of Elleh . Armaments of both types fall into one of four categories: Melee Armaments , Ranged Armaments , Catalysts , and Shields .

title: Bosses
text: | For Elden Ring: Nightreign, seek Nightreign Bosses . Bosses are powerful enemies that can be encountered in Elden Ring and Shadow of the Erdtree . Contents 1 Overview 1.1 Mandatory bosses 1.2 Shardbearer bosses 1.3 Remembrance bosses 1.4 Achievement bosses 2 List of bosses in Elden Ring 3 List of bosses in Shadow of the Erdtree 4 List of Unmarked Bosses 5 Notes 6 See also 7 References

title: Bosses
text: Bosses can be found roaming the overworld or inside one of the many locations in the Lands Between . There are in total 238 bosses. Several key indicators can identify a boss fight. Firstly, the music changes to a more intense or dramatic score. A health bar and the boss's name appear prominently at the bottom of the screen. Bosses yield a larger amount of Runes when defeated, and their items rewards are added directly to the inventory. Unlike common enemies, bosses are not revived when the player rests at a Site of Grace . Some bosses appear out in the open world and will attack the player if they wander too close. These field bosses may be disengaged and fled from. The player can lead them a short distance away from their spawn points. Certain field bosses only appear at night: Deathbirds , Death Rite Birds , and Night's Cavalry .

title: Bosses
text: If the player challenges the dungeon boss, they are trapped within the room by a fog wall until the boss is defeated. If the player is defeated, they may be required to pass through a fog gate to rechallenge the boss. Some difficult bosses may be encountered early on, which can drive player exploration.

title: Bosses
text: Over two hundred bosses are available to fight, but only a few must be beaten to finish the game. These bosses are encountered after acquiring two Great Runes and gaining access to Leyndell, Royal Capital . The boss fights that are not optional and must be beaten to progress include: Any two shardbearers: Godrick , Mohg , Rennala , Rykard , or Radahn Draconic Tree Sentinel or Fia's Champions Godfrey, First Elden Lord Morgott, the Omen King Fire Giant Godskin Duo Maliketh, the Black Blade Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing Godfrey, First Elden Lord Radagon of the Golden Order Elden Beast Two bosses must be defeated in order to access the Shadow of the Erdtree : Starscourge Radahn Mohg, Lord of Blood Similarly, the bosses mandatory for completing the Shadow of the Erdtree are: Messmer the Impaler Romina, Saint of the Bud Promised Consort Radahn

title: Bosses
text: Main article : Shardbearers Two or more Great Runes are required to break a seal and enter Leyndell, Royal Capital . Two Shardbearers must be defeated to complete the story: Godrick the Grafted Mohg, Lord of Blood Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon Rykard, Lord of Blasphemy Starscourge Radahn

title: Bosses
text: Main article : Remembrance Bosses Defeating a Remembrance Boss grants their corresponding Remembrance , which can be traded to Finger Reader Enia for one of two unique items . Additionally, the Remembrance becomes available for duplication at any Wandering Mausoleum or Remembrance Duplication Coffin .

title: Talismans
text: Talismans are special items in Elden Ring . They can be equipped to provide a variety of effects. Note that talismans with variants (like +1 or +2 after the name) will only allow one of these to be equipped at a time, so one can't equip both Arsenal Charm and Arsenal Charm +1 , even if one has enough talisman slots for both. You begin the game with the ability to equip 1 talisman at a time. This number can be increased by acquiring Talisman Pouches .

title: Sorceries
text: | For sorcerers, seek Glintstone Sorcerers . Sorceries (魔術, Majutsu ) are a medium of spells in Elden Ring . All sorceries require some FP to be cast and the majority of them require a heavy investment into the Intelligence stat, however, there are still some Faith and Arcane -based sorceries to be found. Sorceries can only be manifested through the use of Staves acting as a catalyst. The actual damage or effect per casting of a Sorcery is based on a unique stat of Staves called Sorcery Scaling , or SS. The value of this stat for any staff depends on the quality of the staff, its Strengthening level, and a combination of your relevant Stats and Attributes combined with the staff's Grade Ratio for those attributes (primarily focusing on Intelligence).

title: Sorceries
text: Individual spells will have their Power ratio represented as % SS (such as POWER: 75% SS), which represents the percentage of an equipped staff's Sorcery Scaling value that is used for each shot. Do note that enemy resistances will factor into how much damage you see an enemy take; the Power % shown for attack spells is based on calculations done on unarmored neutral critters in the starting zones. Contents 1 Schools of Sorceries 2 List of Sorceries 3 Notes 4 See also

title: Incantations
text: Incantations (祈祷, Kitō ; lit. "Prayers") are a type of spell in Elden Ring . All incantations require FP to cast, with most necessitating a high investment in the Faith stat. However, some incantations are based on Intelligence or Arcane . The Incantation Scaling varies depending on their stat requirements and the player's own stats. Incantations can only be cast using sacred seals , which act as the required catalyst . Contents 1 Schools of Incantation 2 List of incantations 3 Boosting incantations 4 Notes 4.1 Bugs 5 Gallery 6 See also 7 References

title: Incantations
text: The Clawmark Seal and Cinquedea boosts Bestial incantations by 10% each. The Dragon Communion Seal boosts Dragon Communion incantations by 15%. The Dryleaf Seal boosts specific Golden Order and Miquella's incantations by 15%. ( Discus of Light , Multilayered Ring of Light , Radagon's Rings of Light , & Triple Rings of Light only) The Fire Knight's Seal , Messmer's Helm / Messmer's Helm (Altered) , and Fire Knight Armor (not the Altered version) boosts Messmer's Flame incantations by 15%, 3%, and 4% respectively. The Frenzied Flame Seal boosts Frenzied Flame incantations by 20%. The Giant's Seal boosts Giants' Flame incantations by 20%. The incantations Catch Flame , Fire's Deadly Sin , and Flame Sling aren't boosted, as they lack a sigil. The Godslayer's Seal boosts Godslayer incantations (except for Noble Presence ) by 10%. Godskin Noble Robe boosts Noble Presence by 20%.

title: Incantations
text: The Golden Order Seal and Radiant Gold Mask boost Golden Order incantations by 10% each. The Gravel Stone Seal boosts Dragon Cult incantations by 15%. The Spiraltree Seal boosts Spiral incantations by 20%. Each piece of the Crucible Axe Set and Crucible Tree Set boosts Aspects of the Crucible incantations by 3.5% (14% total for a full set). The Divine Bird Helm boosts the effectiveness of Divine Bird Feathers by 10%. The Enraged Divine Beast talisman and Divine Beast Helm / Divine Beast Head boosts the effectiveness of Divine Beast Tornado by 10%. The Pelt of Ralva boosts the effectiveness of Roar of Rugalea by 15%.

title: Incantations
text: Flame of the Fell God , Greyoll's Roar , and Elden Stars are required to complete the Legendary Sorceries and Incantations achievement . No sacred seal boosts Bear Communion, Blood Oath, Divine Beast, Erdtree, Erdtree Worship, Servants of Rot, or Two Fingers incantations. The Erdtree Seal does not boost Erdtree or Erdtree Worship incantations. The Finger Seal does not boost Two Fingers incantations. The following incantations do not produce spell sigils and are unaffected by school-specific boosts: Catch Flame , Divine Beast Tornado , Divine Bird Feathers , Fire's Deadly Sin , Flame Sling , Roar of Rugalea , Placidusax's Ruin , and Watchful Spirit .

title: Agheel's Flame
text: Agheel's Flame is an incantation spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the Dragon Communion Incantations . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effects 4 Notes 5 Trivia 6 See also

title: Agheel's Flame
text: Superior incantation of Dragon Communion . Channels the power of the flying dragon Agheel . Transforms the caster into a dragon to spew flaming breath from above. This incantation can be used while jumping and charging extends the duration. The dead gazed at the skies over the lakes of Limgrave , praying that the dragons' flames would burn them to ash.

title: Agheel's Flame
text: Dragon Communion: Caelid Acquired from the altar at the Cathedral of Dragon Communion in Caelid for 2 Dragon Hearts after defeating Flying Dragon Agheel .

title: Agheel's Flame
text: The caster floats up into the air and summons a dragon head, before breathing out a wave of flames, dealing Fire damage equal to 169% of Incantation Scaling up to 3 times (i.e., 507% of the same cumulatively) if the entire wave hits. Continue channeling the spell to let loose a second breath, also hitting up to 3 times.

title: Agheel's Flame
text: Costs 60 points of Stamina to cast, and another 60 points upon using the follow-up breath. Damage is boosted by 10% by the Roar Medallion . Damage is boosted by 15% per Dragon Communion Seal held in an active hand (2 max). Damage is boosted by 20% when the Rock Heart is used. This spell can be cast midair but cannot be cast while mounted, unlike Dragonfire . Compared to Dragonfire , this spell deals 17% more damage but also consumes 38% more FP . Casting this spell until it stops costs 144 FP: 2*36 FP from the two breaths and 12*6 FP from keeping the spell running. Only 79 FP is actually needed for the entire routine. This is just enough to start the second breath, and thereafter the game does not care that no FP remains as it will continue casting regardless.

title: Agheel's Flame
text: This is the third strongest "named" dragon breath attack, dealing 0.5% less damage than Ghostflame Breath and 10% less damage than Smarag's Glintstone Breath if an enemy's Fire Damage Negation and Magic Damage Negation is equal. Deals 20% less damage against other players.

title: Abductor Virgin
text: Abductor Virgins , also known as Iron Virgins , are Bosses and Enemies in Elden Ring . They are Bosses in Elden Ring Nightreign . Contents 1 Overview 2 Drops 3 Bosses 4 Notes 5 Gallery 6 References

title: Abductor Virgin
text: Abductor Virgins are automatons affiliated with the Volcano Manor . Originally designed with the intent of capturing and transposing victims for interrogation, they can be found strewn around the Lands Between . Though the majority have lost their powers of transposition, they will still attempt to fulfill their original directive if approached. To aid in their task, Abductor Virgins have been outfitted with heavy weaponry on both arms that alter how they approach each target. When a Virgin has approached a target, they will open their chest cavity and attempt to pull them in with serpentine tendrils. Given the loss of their transposition ability, the target will be constricted instead. If they survive this, they will be spat back out.

title: Abductor Virgin
text: They are weak to Lightning damage and all damage taken is greatly increased if attacked when their chest cavity is opened during a charge or attempted grab.

title: Aged One's Exultation
text: A talisman depicting the exultation of the aging untouchable , whose head resembles a sprig of ripe grapes . Raises attack power when madness is triggered in the vicinity. "Gift your madness to our Lord . Bless our brethren with grapes. Take care that they fully ripen."

title: Aged One's Exultation
text: Aged One's Exultation is dropped by the Aging Untouchable found nearest to the Abandoned Church in the Abyssal Woods . The Aging Untouchable is only vulnerable after it has been parried. [ 1 ]

title: Aged One's Exultation
text: Aged One's Exultation increases attack power by +20% for 30 seconds when madness is triggered near the player. This buff may be stacked with Black Dumpling and Madding Hand for a total of +41.9% attack power. This buff is reduced to +12% in PvP. It is highly effective against enemies that induce Madness . Pairs well with builds utilizing frenzy-inducing abilities or spells . The buff persists after removing the talisman.

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Spear
text: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Spear is an incantation spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the Dragon Cult Incantations . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effect 4 Notes 5 Trivia 6 See also

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Spear
text: A secret incantation of the capital's ancient dragon cult . Creates a spear of red lightning and stabs it into the ground from above. On impact, the spear will burst into trails of lightning covering the area. Spoken of in legend, red lightning is the weapon wielded by the ancient dragons .

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Spear
text: Purchase: Roundtable Hold / Liurnia of the Lakes / Altus Plateau / Leyndell, Royal Capital / Mountaintops of the Giants Sold for 8,500 Runes by Brother Corhyn or Miriel, Pastor of Vows after giving either of them the Ancient Dragon Prayerbook . Should the prayerbook be given to one of them and then have that instructor die before purchasing this Incantation , then the player can still purchase it from the Twin Maiden Husks for the same price, after giving them the respective tutor's Bell Bearing . WARNING: Should both Corhyn and Miriel die before giving either of them the prayerbook then this incantation becomes unavailable for that character until a new Journey is started.

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Spear
text: Calls on a red, crackling spear from above that is then slammed into the ground, hitting with Lightning Attack Power equal to 360% of Incantation Scaling in a small 0.2 meter radius, and creating a shockwave with a 3 meter radius that hits with Lightning Attack Power equal to 285% of Incantation Scaling. From the point of impact, waves of red lightning are sent out directly ahead and behind, and to the left and right of the caster with Lightning Attack Power equal to 122% of Incantation Scaling in a 2 meter radius from the center of each wave.

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Spear
text: Casting the spell costs 29 points of Stamina . Deals 17 Stance damage on a direct or shockwave hit and 5 Stance damage on a wave hit. Attack Power is boosted by 15% per Gravel Stone Seal held in an active hand (2 max). Attack Power is boosted by 2% per piece of the Death Knight Set that is being worn. The "direct" hit and the initial area attack cannot hit the same target, though the smaller, weakest waves can hit an enemy that has already been hit if they are either quick or large enough to also be where those are. The "spear" is actually flung downwards, meaning it can be used to hit the ground beneath a cliff if the caster is standing on its edge. Casts faster than Fortissax's Lightning Spear , but is much weaker overall and per hit.

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Spear
text: Like other red lightning incantations, this spell doesn't actually make the caster create a bolt of lightning in the hand, instead summoning the bolt from somewhere above them. Patch 1.07 decreased FP consumption and increased stamina attack power against guarded enemies. FP cost was decreased from 30 to 25. Only the direct hit had its Stamina Attack Power increased against guarding enemies, from 100 to 140. Separate Stamina Attack Power values were added for guarding players: for a direct hit, it was increased from 140 to 175; for a near hit, it was increased from 100 to 125; and for a far away hit, it was increased from 60 to 75. Patch 1.04 included an undocumented change which reduced the FP cost of this incantation. FP cost was decreased from 42 to 30.

title: Ailment Talisman
text: A talisman depicting a soul taken by sickness. When certain ailments are triggered, this talisman grants resistance to the same ailment. When the weak were infected with the dreaded fly sickness , they perished well before the metamorphosis could take hold. Oddly, those who cared for the infected and made certain they were given a proper burial were never afflicted themselves.

title: Ailment Talisman
text: Ailment Talisman is found in the Gravesite Plain at the Abandoned Ailing Village . It is located on a corpse at the edge of an unfinished wooden bridge.

title: Ailment Talisman
text: Ailment Talisman provides ~+350 resistance to a specific status effect after being afflicted. Poison / Scarlet Rot resistance lasts for 120 seconds. Frostbite resistance lasts for 45 seconds. Hemorrhage , Sleep , and Madness resistance last for 30 seconds. Death Blight is not affected, as its onset kills the player. The buildup or duration of the triggering ailment is immediately reduced by a certain amount. This talisman is especially useful in areas or encounters where specific ailments are prevalent.

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Strike
text: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Strike is an incantation spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the Dragon Cult Incantations . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effects 4 Notes 5 Trivia 6 See also

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Strike
text: A secret incantation of the capital's ancient dragon cult . Summons a red bolt of lightning that scatters and spreads around the surrounding area. Charging enhances the range of the lightning bolt. Spoken of in legend, red lightning is the weapon wielded by the ancient dragons .

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Strike
text: Purchase: Roundtable Hold / Liurnia of the Lakes / Altus Plateau / Leyndell, Royal Capital / Mountaintops of the Giants Sold for 10,500 Runes by Brother Corhyn or Miriel, Pastor of Vows after giving either of them the Ancient Dragon Prayerbook . Should the prayerbook be given to one of them and then have that instructor die before purchasing this Incantation , then the player can still purchase it from the Twin Maiden Husks for the same price, after giving them the respective tutor's Bell Bearing . WARNING: Should both Corhyn and Miriel die before giving either of them the prayerbook then this incantation becomes unavailable for that character until a new Journey is started.

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Strike
text: Sends out rows of bolts in all directions, hitting at regular intervals with Lightning Attack Power equal to 317% of Incantation Scaling . Charging the spell sends out additional bolts to extend the range slighly and increases the Attack Power of each hit to 379% of Incantation Scaling.

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Strike
text: Casting the spell costs 31 points of Stamina . Charging the spell increases the cost to 40 points of Stamina. Deals 5 Stance damage per hit. Charging the spell increases this to 9 Stance damage per hit. Attack Power is boosted by 15% by the Godfrey Icon if charged. Since charging the spell already increases Attack Power by 19%, wearing this talisman increases the Attack Power by a total of 37%. Attack Power is boosted by 15% per Gravel Stone Seal held in an active hand (2 max). Attack Power is boosted by 2% per piece of the Death Knight Set that is being worn. If the incantation hits a watery surface or an enemy standing on such a surface, a small area of effect attack with Lightning Attack Power equal to 50% of Incantation Scaling will be created that can hit the target foes and any adjacent enemies.

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Strike
text: Also deals 2 Stance damage regardless of if the original Ancient Dragons' Lightning Strike was charged or not. This is one of the game's strongest attacks, capable of dealing over ten thousand damage to larger bosses such as the Dragonlord or the God-Devouring Serpent , even if they are resistant to Lightning, but even smaller, human-sized enemies will take heavy damage in the thousands if hit by just one or two bolts. This spell sometimes fails to do anything when cast under low-level roofs.

title: Ancient Dragons' Lightning Strike
text: Unlike other red lightning incantations, this spell doesn't summon its bolts bolt from somewhere above them, instead creating them from the used Sacred Seal . Patch 1.04 decreased recovery time. Patch 1.03.3 included an undocumented changed which increased the FP cost of this incantation. FP cost was increased from 27 to 36.

title: Alecto, Black Knife Ringleader
text: Alecto was the ringleader of the Black Knife Assassins , the assailants responsible for the murder of Godwyn the Golden on the Night of Black Knives . [ 1 ] She was believed to be a Numen originating from an Eternal City . [ 2 ] Alecto was said to have had close ties to Queen Marika herself. [ 3 ]

title: Alecto, Black Knife Ringleader
text: After stealing a fragment of Destined Death , Ranni the Witch performed a dark ritual to imbue its power into mishappen blades. [ 4 ] [ 5 ] Alecto and her fellow assassins took the daggers, donned silent, silver scale armor, and made their way toward the Royal Capital . [ 6 ] [ 3 ] [ 2 ] Alecto masked her presence with a concealing veil as she and her co-conspirators entered Leyndell beneath the cover of darkness. [ 7 ] When the moment was right, they carved the Cursemark of Death into Godwyn's flesh and slew his soul. Alecto's daughter, Tiche , died protecting her during their ensuing flight from the capital. [ 1 ] Alecto herself was later captured and sealed within an Evergaol by an unknown party. [ 8 ]

title: Ancestral Spirit's Horn
text: Item cut from the horns of the Regal Ancestor Spirit . Restore FP upon defeating enemies . A number of new growths bud from the antler-like horns of the fallen king , each glowing with light . Thus does new life grow from death, and from death , one obtains power.

title: Ancestral Spirit's Horn
text: Trade the Remembrance of the Regal Ancestor , gained by defeating Regal Ancestor Spirit , with Finger Reader Enia . Alternatively, use one of the Wandering Mausoleums to duplicate one (can only obtain one per playthrough using either method).

title: Ancestral Spirit's Horn
text: Gain 3 FP when an enemy dies.

title: Ancestral Spirit's Horn
text: The FP gain effect is signified by small blue swirls entering the character's body. Stacks with the similar effects from the Sacrificial Axe and Sword of Milos , for a possible 12 FP gain per death. The enemy doesn't have to be killed by the wearer of this Talisman for the effect to take place, nor do the killed enemies have to be hostile to the wearer. This makes it useful for Invaders that want to hang back and recuperate after an unsuccessful attempt, or when harassing the Host and Fingers/Summons with ranged attacks, Spells , or Skills . Fairly useful in the open world and dungeons for the FP regain effect lowering the need to use the Flask of Cerulean Tears to replenish it, but usually useless in boss fights as the vast majority of them do not have easily killable minions to activate it.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Bloom
text: Aspect of the Crucible discovered in the ancient ruins . Creates a miranda flower on one's chest before calling down a rain of light. Charging increases potency. In an age long past, before this land was enshrouded in shadow, the vitality of the Crucible flourished.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Bloom
text: Aspects of the Crucible: Bloom is found in the upper sections of the Ancient Ruins of Rauh . From the Rauh Ancient Ruins, East site of grace , head across the bridge just to the west, then drop down to a slightly lower bridge section at the end.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Bloom
text: Sprouts a flower from the user's chest, creating a rain of light around them that lasts for 4 seconds and has Holy Attack Power equal to 80% of Incantation Scaling per hit. Charging the spell increases the Attack Power per hit to 100% of Incantation Scaling.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Bloom
text: Costs 20 points of Stamina to cast. Charging the spell increases the cost to 26 points of Stamina. Attack Power is boosted by wearing pieces of the Crucible Set (4% per piece, 17% for the whole set because 1.04^4 = 1.17). Attack Power is boosted by 15% by the Godfrey Icon if charged. Since charging the spell already increases Attack Power by 25%, wearing this talisman increases Attack Power by a total of 44%. Can deal decent damage against large foes such as Dragons , but more mobile or smaller foes will likely only be hit once or twice for minimal damage. There is about a 2-second delay before the rain of light begins after casting the spell.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Bloom
text: This spell is very similar to the light spell used by Miranda Blossoms , Melina , and the cut Miranda's Prayer tool . However, this spell creates a flower at the beginning of its cast. Patch 1.14 increased attack speed, increased the size of the hitbox, and shortened the recovery time between using this incantation and follow-up actions.

title: Ancestor Spirit
text: The antler-like horns of a long-lived stag continued budding long after death, over and over again, until the beast became an Ancestor Spirit. Ancestral Spirits are a phenomenon that exists beyond the purview of the Erdtree , following the principle that life sprouts from death, just as it does birth, granting power. Its horned remains are found at the southern Hallowhorn Grounds on the bank of the Siofra River , where it lies beneath the false night sky. Its large, antler-like horns sprouted small leafy plants that shone with light, infusing nearby Ancestral Followers with profound spirituality. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ] [ 4 ]

title: Ancestor Spirit
text: This boss is optional. Furled Fingers or Spirit Ashes can be summoned for this boss. Deals Standard and Magic damage

title: Ancestor Spirit
text: The horned remains of this Ancestor Spirit are found at the Hallowhorn Grounds in the lower section of the Siofra River , accessible via the Siofra River Well in Limgrave . This ruined structure is situated southeast of the Siofra River Bank Site of Grace . In order to face this boss, the player must first light eight beacons scattered throughout the area. Each beacon corresponds to a pillar flanking the staircase to the Hallowhorn Grounds. Once all signals are lit, the following message displays: Power gathers somewhere in horned remains . The horned remains will burned with blue spectral flames, and agreeing to Touch horned remains will grant a supernatural encounter with the spirit.

text: Briars of Punishment is a sorcery spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the thorn sorceries . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effects 4 Notes 4.1 Boosting 5 Strategies 6 Trivia 7 See also

title: Briars of Punishment
text: An aberrant sorcery discovered by exiled criminals . Theirs are the sorceries most reviled by the academy . Wounds the caster with thorns of punishment , sending a trail of bloodthorns running over the ground to impale enemies from below. This sorcery can be cast repeatedly. The guilty , their eyes gouged by thorns , lived in eternal darkness. There, they discovered the blood star .

title: Briars of Punishment
text: Loot: Mountaintops of the Giants Located by a dead tree on the western cliffs of the southern end of the giant stone bridge surrounded by Guilty enemies.

title: Briars of Punishment
text: The caster sacrifices their own blood to summon crawling vines of bloodthorn that pursue a single target. This spell deals Magic damage with an Attack Power equal to 212% of Sorcery Scaling , inflicting 72 Blood Loss and 7 Stance damage.

title: Briars of Punishment
text: Costs 50 HP and applies 25 Blood Loss per cast or recast. Costs 9 FP per cast or recast. Costs 22 Stamina per cast or recast. This spell has a long range and can scale walls and other obstacles. This spell will track and home in on its target. This spell utilizes the Briars of Sin .

title: Briars of Punishment
text: Alberich's Bracers , Alberich's Robe , Alberich's Pointed Hat , and Alberich's Pointed Hat (Altered) each boost the effectiveness by 6%, up to a maximum of 18%. Staff of the Guilty boosts the Attack Power by 20%. Magic Scorpion Charm increases Magic Attack Power by 12% (8% in PvP). Magic-Shrouding Cracked Tear increases Magic Attack Power by 20% (15% in PvP) for 3 minutes. Albinauric Staff and Maternal Staff provide a scaling bonus to Blood Loss buildup based on the caster's Arcane attribute. Azur's Glintstone Staff reduces the casting time of spells. Lord of Blood's Exultation boosts attack power by 20% (12% in PvP) for 20 seconds when Blood Loss occurs nearby.

title: Briars of Punishment
text: Briars of Punishment fires fewer projectiles than Impenetrable Thorns but is more reliable at long range against single targets.

title: Briars of Punishment
text: Requires Faith rather than Intelligence . Patch 1.07 reduced the FP cost from 13 to 9, increased the on-hit Blood Loss from 60 to 72, and reduced the casting time.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Breath
text: One of the ancient Erdtree incantations . Creates a throat pouch on the caster's neck, allowing them to spew fiery breath while walking. Charging increases the duration of the breath. This is a manifestation of the Erdtree 's primal vital energies—an aspect of the primordial crucible , where all life was once blended together.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Breath
text: Aspects of the Crucible: Breath is acquired upon defeating Tanith's Knight , which can be fought after killing Tanith in Rykard 's arena in the Volcano Manor , at the end of her questline.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Breath
text: Creates a large pouch on the caster's neck before breathing out tongues of flame with Fire Attack Power equal to 98% of Incantation Scaling .

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Breath
text: Costs 40 points of Stamina to cast. Attack Power is boosted by 10% by the Roar Medallion . Attack Power is boosted by wearing pieces of the Crucible Set (4% per piece, 17% for the whole set because 1.04^4 = 1.17). Doesn't immediately start to consume FP for the upkeep cost, instead only starting after about 2-3 seconds. This seems to coincide with the initially created throat pouch shrinking to roughly the width of the character's torso. The caster must always move forward at a walking pace as long as this spell is being channeled. Decent in PvP as a "wake-up" attack (i.e., after an attack that has knocked down an opponent, such as a Critical Attack ), since it has a relatively short start-up animation and a wide attack area, which can cause the additional damage needed to finish off a target.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Breath
text: This spell is used by Crucible Axe Knights (except for the two found in Limgrave ). Aspects of the Crucible: Breath creates a throat pouch similar to those of the basilisks .

text: Lansseax, sister of Fortissax , was an Ancient Dragon that took the form of a human priestess to commune with the knights of Leyndell 's Ancient Dragon Cult . [ 1 ] Her most beloved knight was Vyke the Dragonspear , [ 2 ] a Tarnished knight of the Roundtable Hold , who was later scorched by the Frenzied Flame . [ 3 ]

title: Ancient Dragon Lansseax
text: Lansseax is first encountered near the Abandoned Coffin Site of Grace , ambushing the player near the entrance to Altus Plateau from the Ruin-Strewn Precipice . After sustaining enough damage, she abruptly ends the fight by flying away. Her next encounter is on a hill southwest of the Rampartside Path Site of Grace, located in eastern Altus Plateau, where she can be defeated for good.

title: Arrow's Soaring Sting Talisman
text: Can be found on the top of a tower at Fog Rift Fort . From within the door of the tower where Black Knight Garrew was, go through a gap between the tower and the rock wall to the east of the doorway. Hop down onto the wooden pathway and follow it until you see a ladder on your right. Take it up, after which you'll see another ladder on your left. Take it to the uppermost level of the tower. Here you can find the treasure chest containing the Talisman.

text: An aberrant sorcery discovered by exiled criminals . Theirs are the sorceries most reviled by the academy . Wounds the caster with thorns of sin , creating a spiral of bloodthorns . This sorcery can be cast repeatedly, up to three times. The guilty , their eyes gouged by thorns , lived in eternal darkness. There, they discovered the blood star .

title: Briars of Sin (spell)
text: Guaranteed Drop: Liurnia of the Lakes Obtained by defeating a lone Guilty located southwest of the Fire Monk Campsite , near the Church of Vows in Liurnia. This Sorcerer is near a group of Fire Slugs to the north of the Artist's Shack (Liurnia) .

title: Briars of Sin (spell)
text: The caster sacrifices their own blood to summon swirling vines of bloodthorn around themselves. This spell can be recast up to 2 additional times, with increasing intensity. It deals Magic damage with an Attack Power equal to 200/232/233% of Sorcery Scaling within a 2.5/3.5/4.5-meter radius, inflicting 95/95/95 Blood Loss and 7/7.5/8 Stance damage.

title: Briars of Sin (spell)
text: Costs 80 HP and applies 30 Blood Loss per cast or recast. Costs 6 FP per cast or recast. Costs 26 Stamina per cast or recast. This spell can be recast up to 3 times with additional inputs.

title: Briars of Sin (spell)
text: Alberich's Bracers , Alberich's Robe , Alberich's Pointed Hat , and Alberich's Pointed Hat (Altered) each boost the Attack Power by 6%, up to a maximum of 18%. Staff of the Guilty boosts the attack power by 20%. Magic Scorpion Charm increases Magic attack power by 12% (8% in PvP). Magic-Shrouding Cracked Tear increases Magic attack power by 20% (15% in PvP) for 3 minutes. Albinauric Staff and Maternal Staff provide a scaling bonus to Blood Loss buildup based on the caster's Arcane attribute. Azur's Glintstone Staff reduces the casting time of spells. Lord of Blood's Exultation boosts attack power by 20% (12% in PvP) for 20 seconds when Blood Loss occurs nearby.

title: Briars of Sin (spell)
text: This spell produces no sigil when cast. Used by Mad Tongue Alberich and the Guilty Sorcerers . Requires Faith rather than Intelligence . Update 1.07 reduced the FP cost from 9 to 6. The Briars of Sin had a different icon during the Closed Network Test for Elden Ring . It featured the spell sigil for Giants' Flame Incantations .

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Horns
text: Aspects of the Crucible: Horns is an incantation spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the Erdtree incantations . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effect 4 Notes 5 Trivia 6 See also

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Horns
text: One of the ancient Erdtree incantations . Creates a mighty horn on the caster's shoulder to gore foes from a low stance. Charging allows the caster to barrel into foes before delivering the final attack. This is a manifestation of the Erdtree 's primal vital energies—an aspect of the primordial crucible , where all life was once blended together.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Horns
text: Aspects of the Crucible: Horns is acquired by defeating the Crucible Knight in Stormveil Castle , down the outer western rampart. From the Rampart Tower Site of Grace, head north to the walkway with the Warhawks , and drop down to the left at the guard tower. Continue dropping down until you reach a breakable cliff and fall through it to arrive on a cliffside with the Crucible Knight.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Horns
text: The caster crouches slightly and creates a horn on their shoulder before dashing a few steps forward, hitting with Standard Attack Power equal to 277% of Incantation Scaling upon contact with the horn. Charging the spell increases the Attack Power to 327% of Incantation Scaling and greatly increases the distance charged, to about three times the previous amount, launching any human-sized foes that are hit.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Horns
text: Costs 38 points of Stamina to cast. Charging the spell increases the cost to 49 points of Stamina. Attack Power is boosted by wearing pieces of the Crucible Set (4% per piece, 17% for the whole set because 1.04^4 = 1.17). Attack Power is boosted by 15% by the Godfrey Icon if charged. Since charging the spell already increases Attack Power by 18%, wearing this talisman increases Attack Power by a total of 36%. Deals 16 Stance damage when uncharged and 30 Stance damage when charged. The charged version of the spell is generally much better than the uncharged version, primarily due to the increased distance traveled and the ability to launch smaller foes into the air.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Horns
text: This spell is used by Crucible Tree Knights , though they summon it on their arm. Despite this, the spell is dropped by a Crucible Axe Knight. Patch 1.07 decreased FP consumption and increased stamina attack power against guarded enemies. FP cost was reduced from 20 to 18, and damage to Stamina of guarding enemies was increased from 200 to 240 for the uncharged version and from 260 to 300 for the charged version. Patch 1.04 decreased FP and Stamina cost, increased cast speed, increased the distance traveled when not charged, and made it easier to cause enemies to flinch when charged. FP cost was reduced from 30 to 20.

text: Cannon of Haima is a spell in Elden Ring . A powerful artillery-like blast with a shot trajectory similar to a Flame Sling , but creates a large high-damage explosion at the impact point. Devastating to clustered groups of enemies.

title: Cannon of Haima

text: Converted Fringe Tower , north-east Liurnia of the Lakes , not to be mistaken with the Converted Tower in the south-west. You will need to use the Erudition gesture given by Thops in front of the statue of Marika . This will reveal a hidden ladder to the top of the tower with a treasure chest that contains the spell. Unlike with the Converted Tower, here you will not only have to do the gesture, but also wear a glintstone crown headpiece while doing so. Patch: 1.07, Changes: FP cost reduced from 45 to 38.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Tail
text: One of the ancient Erdtree incantations. Creates a supple tail that sweeps through foes before the caster. Charging enhances potency. This is a manifestation of the Erdtree 's primal vital energies - an aspect of the primordial crucible , where all life was once blended together.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Tail
text: Aspects of the Crucible: Tail is acquired by defeating the Crucible Knight trapped in the Stormhill Evergaol , located in Stormhill .

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Tail
text: Creates a tail that swings in front of the caster with Standard Attack Power equal to 202% of Incantation Scaling . If charged, it adds a second, larger swing, dealing Standard Attack Power equal to 274% of Incantation Scaling .

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Tail
text: Costs 42 points of Stamina to cast, whether charged or uncharged. Attack Power is boosted by wearing pieces of the Crucible Set (4% per piece, 17% for the whole set because 1.04^4 = 1.17). Attack Power is boosted by 15% by the Godfrey Icon if charged. Since charging the spell already increases Attack Power by 36%, wearing this talisman increases Attack Power by a total of 56%. The first swing deals 13 Stance damage, and the second swing deals 39 Stance damage. This means that the charged version of the spell can deal up to 52 Stance damage, greater than even a hammer-type Colossal Weapon 's charged heavy attack, allowing it to break the stance of many enemies within two hits. The spell goes through obstacles, making it decent for ambushing foes by using thin walls as cover. It is a bit slow to cast and has about a second of recovery time after the spell finishes casting.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Tail
text: Launches smaller foes backward if it hits them.

FP cost was reduced from 22 to 20, and stamina damage to guarding enemies was increased from 180 to 200 on the first swipe and from 240 to 270 on the second swipe. Patch 1.04 decreased FP and Stamina cost. FP cost was reduced from 32 to 22.

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: The Ancient Dragon-Man is enountered twice in the Gravesite Plain —once as an Invader and once as a formal boss. His Invasion triggers on the road southwest of the Pillar Path Waypoint Site of Grace . He is fought up the hill past Igon , who audibly cowers on the ground. The boss version is encountered at the bottom of the Dragon's Pit. Defeating him opens the path to the Foot of the Jagged Peak , which connects to Charo's Hidden Grave and the Jagged Peak . Formerly a drake warrior who hunted colossal dragons , the Ancient Dragon-Man partook in the original form of Dragon Communion by consuming a jagged Rock Heart . [ 1 ] He took on the stony form of a small, bipedal ancient dragon and became the arbiter of those worthy to devour Bayle the Dread . [ 2 ]

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: Wields the Dragon-Hunter's Great Katana and Dragon Communion Seal . Uses the Magma Breath and Dragonmaw Incantations . Prefers two-handed jumping attacks and Dragonwound Slash . Has transformed using a Rock Heart .

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: Ancient Dragon-Man has a single Flask of Crimson Tears . He has 0 Poise and 0% Damage Negation for each Damage Type . Draconic Tree Sentinels also became dragons to better fight dragons. [ 3 ]

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: It is possible to defeat the Ancient Dragon-Man without having leveled up or upgraded one's Weapons , Flasks and even Scadutree Blessings with relative comfort through the use of spamming the crouching light attack from Whips . As its long reach, fast attack-speed, low stamina-consumption and early stamina-regeneration, combined with the Ancient Dragon-Man's non-existent poise, allows the player to consistently harass him from a relatively safe distance without needing to worry about him trying to excessively dodge and then rush the player down like conventional projectiles from spells or bows would. The player can increase their un-upgraded weapon's damage-output through the use of Blood Loss , Frostbite and Poison procs that are then immediately followed by the use of the Skill , The Poison Flower Blooms Twice as a third source of blood loss-like burst-damage.

title: Ancient Dragon-Man
text: The most efficient set-up for this would be to have two different whips they can alternate between. Hoslow's Petal Whip and Thorned Whip are excellent choices as they can both have their Affinities changed through the use of Ashes of War , while at the same time preserving their innate blood loss buildup no matter what affinity is chosen. So one Poison whip with Ash of War: The Poison Flower Blooms Twice and a Cold whip with almost any ash of the user's choosing.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Thorns
text: Aspect of the Crucible that manifested in golden hippopotami . Creates a bristling mass of thorn-like fur on the caster's back before scouring the area by rolling around on the ground. Charging increases the number of rolls and range of attack. In an age long past, before this land was enshrouded in shadow, the vitality of the Crucible flourished. Remains of this can be seen in the ancient ruins .

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Thorns
text: Aspects of the Crucible: Thorns is acquired by defeating the Golden Hippopotamus boss found just inside the main gate of Shadow Keep on Scadu Altus .

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Thorns
text: Sprouts spines from the back and rolls sideways on the ground, throwing them around the user, dealing Pierce Attack Power equal to 45% of Incantation Scaling per hit, hitting up to 10 times (450% of Incantation Scaling in total). [verification needed] The spell can be charged to spin twice, increasing the maximum number of hits to 15 and moving forward. These extra attacks deal Attack Power equal to 120% of Incantation Scaling per hit (600% of Incantation Scaling for the extra hits or 1000% of Incantation Scaling in total).

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Thorns
text: Costs 29 points of Stamina to cast. Charging the spell increases the cost to 38 points of Stamina. Attack Power is boosted by wearing pieces of the Crucible Set (4% per piece, 17% for the whole set because 1.04^4 = 1.17). Attack Power is boosted by 15% by the Godfrey Icon if charged. Since charging the spell already increases Attack Power by 122%, wearing this talisman increases Attack Power by a total of 156%. The very low height of the user when casting this spell means that high-centered thrusting or horizontal attacks can easily miss them. The charged version's increased forward momentum makes it even better at evading attacks. Since this Incantation deals Pierce damage, it will occasionally deal a Counter Hit against an attacking foe, increasing the Attack Power of that specific hit by 30%.

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Thorns
text: Note that this will most likely result in an overall 2-3% damage increase per Counter Hit since each is only a portion of the total damage. Since this isn't a weapon, it does NOT benefit from the Spear Talisman .

title: Aspects of the Crucible: Thorns
text: Patch 1.14 increased attack power and stamina attack power against guarding enemies. The close-range hitbox now follows the movement of the player. The attack power was increased from 40% of Incantation Scaling to 45% for the uncharged hits and most of the charged hits, while the increase was from 70% to 120% of Incantation Scaling for the stronger charged hits. Stamina attack power against guarding enemies was only increased for the charged hits, going from 50 to 85 against PvE enemies and from 62 to 106 against other players.

title: Claws
text: Claws are a type of melee armament in Elden Ring .

title: Claws
text: Claws are hand-held weapons consisting of one or more blades extending from the knuckles of the wielder. They are similar to Fists in terms of their attack patterns and range, though they scale primarily with Dexterity rather than Strength . Though they are shorter than most blades (ranging between that of Daggers and Straight Swords ) and deal modest damage per hit, Claws excel at delivering rapid-fire, close-range slashing attacks that rend flesh easily. With the exception of the Venomous Fang, all Claws feature innate Bleed Damage. Each Claw comes in a set of two, with the Tarnished wielding one on each hand when equipping a Claw "two-handed" without taking up both hand slots. When equipped one-handed, the Tarnished will hold one claw in one hand while the other is left tied to their belt.

title: Claws
text: + Lightweight + Fast/high DPS + Can proc Blood Loss and other status effects quickly (if applied) + Paired Weapons - can be wielded in both hands with only one slot - Short Reach - Only four kinds available/cannot be farmed within the same playthrough (I.E. if a player wants to dual-wield two sets of the same type of Claws, they will have to wait until NG+ to acquire another) - Few Ashes of War available

Has a slight delay between the casting time and the time it is swung so it will need to be timed carefully to avoid getting hit first (cancelling it), but damage is decent, Stance damage is very good and it can hit enemies through walls.

title: Assassin's Approach
text: Assassin's Approach is an incantation spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the Two Fingers incantations . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effects 4 Notes 4.1 Strategies 5 Trivia 6 See also

title: Assassin's Approach
text: Incantation of the Two Fingers' servants, who once served as the assassins of the Roundtable Hold . Completely silences the footsteps of the caster. Additionally, reduces fall damage and the sound produced by falling. This incantation can be cast while in motion or crouching. The assassins were charged with eliminating Tarnished who had strayed from guidance .

title: Assassin's Approach
text: Purchase: Roundtable Hold / Liurnia of the Lakes / Altus Plateau / Leyndell, Royal Capital / Mountaintops of the Giants Starting spell for the Confessor origin . Sold for 2,000 Runes by Brother Corhyn or Miriel, Pastor of Vows after giving either of them the Assassin's Prayerbook . Should the prayerbook be given to one of them and then that instructor die before purchasing this Incantation , the player can still purchase it from the Twin Maiden Husks for the same price, after giving them the respective tutor's Bell Bearing . WARNING: Should both Corhyn and Miriel die before giving either of them the prayerbook, this incantation becomes unavailable for that character until a new Journey is started.

title: Assassin's Approach
text: Assassin's Approach applies a Buff that silences footsteps and prevents all non-lethal fall damage for 30 seconds.

title: Assassin's Approach
text: Costs 15 FP per cast. Costs 13 Stamina per cast. Requires 1 Memory Slot to be equipped. Non-lethal fall damage is only negated for falls between 16 and 20 meters, meaning this incantation does not prevent guaranteed deaths from falls of 20 meters or more. Overrides and is overridden by Soft Cotton .

title: Assassin's Approach
text: Generally outclassed by Soft Cotton , which is an easy-to-acquire consumable tool that costs no FP to activate. However, Soft Cotton lasts only 20 seconds and cannot be crafted during combat.

title: Assassin's Approach
text: Update 1.03 (undocumented) increased FP cost from 12 to 15. Update 1.07 extended the duration of this spell from 20 seconds to 30 seconds.

title: Colossal Swords
text: Colossal Swords are a type of melee armament in Elden Ring .

title: Colossal Swords
text: They have excellent reach and area coverage, along with high damage against both health and poise , at the cost of their high weight and slow attack speed. Its one-handed moveset's slow speed is at risk of getting parried; however, most moves of its two-handed moveset are completely un-parriable. Most Colossal Swords require a decent amount of investment into the Strength attribute in order to be wielded; however, it's possible to still wield a colossal sword with less strength than listed through two-handing, as two-handing increases the player's effective Strength by 50%.

title: Barrier of Gold
text: One of the incantations of Erdtree Worship. Greatly increases magic damage negation for the caster and nearby allies. Hold to continue praying and delay activation. This incantation was used by the champions of the Erdtree in the First and the Second Liurnian Wars , during which the red-haired Radagon joined the heroes' ranks.

title: Barrier of Gold
text: Guaranteed Drop: Leyndell, Royal Capital Drops from an invisible Scarab rolling back and forth on the path patrolled by a Crucible Tree Knight , north of the Erdtree Sanctuary Site of Grace , past the elevator downwards. WARNING: If Maliketh, the Black Blade is defeated before this incantation is acquired it becomes unavailable for that character until a new Journey is started.

title: Barrier of Gold
text: Grants a Body Buff that decreases Magic damage taken by 60% (20% in PvP) for 70 seconds for caster and allies within a 6 meter radius.

title: Barrier of Gold
text: Costs 13 points of Stamina to cast. Overall a very strong defensive incantation, if one that comes a bit late and will most likely come after the Magic-heavy area of Liurnia of the Lakes , with only the Astels and Loretta being the major Magic damage bosses left. Since it doesn't actually apply a flat increase to damage negation, just the damage taken, the actual effective new damage negation (the one shown on the character screen) can be calculated with the following formula: 1 - (1 - (current Magic damage negation)/100) * (1 - 0.60). As an example, if Magic damage negation before casting the incantation is 25.000 the result becomes this: 1 - (1 - 25.000/100) * (1 - 0.60) = 0.7, or 70.0% Magic damage negation. Against other players, simply replace the 0.60 with 0.20 for a new end result of 40.0% Magic damage negation.

title: Barrier of Gold
text: Since it's effectively the damage taken that is reduced, the higher Magic damage negation stat a character has before casting the spell the less effective it may appear to be in the statistics.

title: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
text: Far beyond the Lands Between , within the Greater Will 's lightless abyss , the many-colored debris of ancient stars coalesced into a malformed creature. [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ] A falling star of ill omen, Astel arrived long ago. [ 4 ] It assailed the Eternal Cities with a barrage of meteors, [ 5 ] [ 6 ] [ 7 ] sealing them deep underground, away from the true night sky. [ 8 ] It destroyed the Nameless Eternal City and Noklateo, the Shrouded City . [ 9 ] [ 10 ] [ 11 ]

title: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
text: A procession of multi-colored orbs, Astel is an aberrant, cosmic entity that resembles a Great Dragonfly . [ 12 ] It has a humanoid skull with large mandibles and a large blue eye emerging from a crack in its forehead. It has four translucent insect wings and six skeletal arms that end in human-like hands, the forelimbs each possessing six fingers . Its long tail ends in a spiny scorpion stinger, wielded as a flail . It also casts gravity -based magic and can warp to other parts of the arena.

title: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
text: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void, is encountered within a secluded, starlit cavern somewhere along the underground Ainsel River , beyond the Lake of Rot . This area is accessed by entering the Transportation Coffin overlooking a chasm at the Grand Cloister . The elevator Astel blocks is the only path to the hidden Moonlight Altar , a necessary step for Ranni the Witch 's questline.

title: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
text: Defeating Astel, Naturalborn of the Void for the first time unlocks the " Astel, Naturalborn of the Void " achievement. Defeating this boss is optional, though it is mandatory for the " Age of the Stars " ending. Astel resembles a Great Dragonfly . Astel, Naturalborn of the Void, is the boss of the Grand Cloister, an area populated by insect-like Kindred of Rot .

title: Astel, Naturalborn of the Void
text: This boss was named Astel, Misbegotten of the Void in version 1.00 . At the time, the Misbegotten were referred to as "Aberrations." Astel has an unused animation in which it fell from the sky, crashing to the earth like a meteor. This move is used by Starscourge Radahn . The internal data Astel's boss arena includes spawn markers for an Ancestor Spirit and its spirit animals, suggesting the Grand Cloister boss was reassigned to Astel from a third Ancestor Spirit encounter. It is also possible that one of the Ancestor Spirit boss rooms was duplicated and used as a template.

title: Bayle's Flame Lightning
text: One of the greatest Dragon Communion incantations . Harnesses the fearsome power of Bayle the Dread . Channels the form of the dread dragon in the right arm to smash foes with an exposed talon bone coursing with flame lightning . Even after being consumed, the throbbing heart of Bayle continues to resist its subjugation, never weakening. One day, the fire within will consume the very body and soul of its Communion devourer. One day.

title: Bayle's Flame Lightning
text: Summons one of Bayle's arms to slam down with, causing an explosion around the point of impact. A direct hit deals Standard damage equal to 250% of Incantation Scaling , Lightning damage equal to 280% of the same, and Fire damage equal to 220% of the same, while an enemy that isn't directly hit but moves into the point of impact afterward is dealt Lightning damage equal to 200% of Incantation Scaling and Fire damage equal to 150% of the same. Enemies just outside of the hit area take Fire damage equal to 30% of Incantation Scaling and Lightning damage equal to 15% of the same.

title: Bayle's Flame Lightning
text: Damage is boosted by 15% per Dragon Communion Seal held in an active hand (2 max). Damage is boosted by 20% when the Rock Heart is used. Has some Hyperarmor when cast, and can thus resist a hit or two from most foes before being knocked down on the ground. While it does partially do Lightning damage, it won't cause the same spreading aftershocks on water that other Lightning damage spells do.

text: Axe Talisman is a talisman in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effect 4 Notes 5 Trivia 6 See also

title: Axe Talisman
text: A talisman depicting an axe and a warrior . Enhances charge attacks. The Lord who led the Long March bore an axe , and his loyal warriors honored him by wielding axes of their own, making them very effective at dealing decisive blows.

title: Axe Talisman
text: Obtained from a chest in the cellar of Mistwood Ruins , eastern Limgrave . A Runebear lies very close to the entrance.

title: Axe Talisman
text: Increases the damage of charged heavy melee attacks by 10%.

title: Axe Talisman
text: Doesn't enhance the Dark Moon Greatsword's charged heavy attacks when they're buffed by its Unique Skill: Moonlight Greatsword . Doesn't work with the projectiles launched by the charged heavy attacks of the Frozen Needle or Wing of Astel . Has strong synergy with the Roar Medallion if using a skill that boosts heavy attacks. Skill compatible with both are Barbaric Roar , Braggart's Roar , Shriek of Milos , and War Cry . Synergy with the Spear Talisman with weapons that have charged heavy attacks that deal Pierce damage. Weapon types that primarily have thrusting heavy attacks are Great Spears , Spears (except for the Death Ritual Spear , Partisan , and Spiked Spear ), Heavy Thrusting Swords (except for the Great Épée ), Thrusting Swords (except for the Estoc ), and Straight Swords (except for the Broadsword , Cane Sword , Carian Knight's Sword , and Lazuli Glintstone Sword ).

title: Axe Talisman
text: Other notable weapons are the Claymore , Lucerne , Nagakiba , Pickaxe , Rusted Anchor , Warpick , and the Zweihander and Troll's Golden Sword . Works well with the unique charged heavy attack of the Iron Cleaver and Celebrant's Cleaver , since while they are weaker than the usual Axe charged heavy attack they also include a run-up that potentially let's them be used from a safe distance to close in.

Benefits from boosts to both Fire damage and Lightning damage, though more so the former than the latter.

title: Crystal Sword
text: Crystal Sword is a straight sword found in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Notes 4 Upgrade Information 5 See Also

title: Crystal Sword
text: Sword fashioned from pure crystal ; a deed impossible for a human . Enwreathed with powerful magic , its attack scales with intelligence . The inscrutable Crystalians have but one clear purpose, to safeguard their crystals unto the end. One theory posits that they yearn for the return of their creator who will carve for them new brethren.

title: Crystal Sword
text: Found after the bridge in the Village of the Albinaurics Site of Grace , located in Liurnia of the Lakes .

title: Crystal Sword
text: The skill, Spinning Slash , is potentially quite damaging, especially if it catches another player offguard and with low Poise , though it's somewhat hampered by the lack of reach on Straight Swords. Does best as a pure Strength weapon, as Dexterity and Intelligence are somewhat hampered by having much lower scaling numbers and being based off the lower Magic base damage respectively. For comparison (unmentioned stats are at the minimum required level): 80 Strength grants 652 Attack Power (AP), 45 Strength and 45 Intelligence grants 632 AP, 45 Strength and 45 Dexterity grants 624 AP, and 80 Intelligence grants 560 AP. A good offhand weapon for those wishing to dual-wield Straight Swords on a Strength build with some Intelligence, due to the ease of upgrading weapons reliant on Somber Smithing Stones and its somewhat mediocre skill not mattering there.

title: Battlemage
text: Scholars of the Haima Conspectus of the Academy of Raya Lucaria . Haima scholars are the adjudicators of the Academy, [ 1 ] who studied sorceries of cannon and gavel in order to gain the power to quell conflict. [ 2 ] Whenever war broke out and the Academy sealed its gates, the battlemages of Haima were the only scholars who dared venture outside. [ 3 ] As such, they can be found in many regions of the Lands Between . The stone clubs they carry reflect their more forceful approach, allowing them to feel the impact of their blows so they do not lose sight of the true essence of battle.

title: Carian Slicer
text: One of the sorceries of the Carian royal family . Conjures a magic sword and delivers a swift sweeping slash. This sorcery can be used without delay after performing another action. Even among the Carian sword sorceries, this is a notably effective technique.

title: Carian Slicer

Should the scroll be given to one of them and then have that instructor die before purchasing this Sorcery , then the player can still purchase it from the Twin Maiden Husks for the same price, after giving them the respective tutor's Bell Bearing . WARNING: Should all three of Sellen, Miriel, and Seluvis die before giving any of them the scroll then this sorcery becomes unavailable for that character until a new Journey is started.

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Bayle the Dread was a legendary black dragon who is said to be the ancestor of the drakes or lesser dragons. Long ago, Bayle betrayed the Ancient dragons and clashed with Dragonlord Placidusax . [ 1 ] Their vicious battle ended in stalemate and left both dragons with grievous injuries and loss of body parts, with Bayle losing his two winged arms, his pair of wings on his back and his left leg, with the dreaded dragon fleeing to the Jagged Peak soon afterwards. [ 2 ] [ 3 ]

title: Bayle the Dread
text: As punishment for his betrayal, Placidusax created the art of Dragon Communion , sending his followers to shepherd humanity into hunting Bayle and his descendants. [ 4 ] As reward for felling the drakes, the dragon hunters consumed their hearts in order to gain their power.  Bayle was pursued by Florissax , a servant of Placidusax who sought to recruit mortals in the hunt for Bayle. [ 5 ] [ 6 ] Dragonslayer Igon , a particularly obsessive drake warrior, was one such mortal. Igon faced Bayle, but was left broken in both body and spirit by their battle. [ 7 ] [ 8 ]

title: Bayle the Dread
text: The gravelstone heart of Bayle Igon was stumbled upon by a Tarnished champion , and begged them to summon his spirit to face Bayle once more. Together, the pair succeed in slaying Bayle. [ 9 ] [ 10 ] If Bayle's heart is eaten at the Grand Altar of Dragon Communion , his willpower will one day consume the body and soul of the drake warrior. [ 11 ]

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Bayle is weakest against piercing damage, Dragon Communion Grease , and Igon's Harpoons .

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Bayle is stuck on the ground. He commands a unique power that combines fire and lightning damage. He is a

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Bayle summons an extra set of wings made from red lightning. He is able to fly.

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Two of Placidusax's lost heads can be found still clinging onto the upside of Bayle's neck. The ancient dragons who sided with Bayle against Placidusax wielded the branching red lightning . [ 12 ] Drake Warrior Igon may be summoned inside the area if the player has received his Furled Finger . Igon and Florissax will reward the player after Bayle is defeated. Bayle is the only boss in the game who displays the text 'Legend Felled' when defeated who does not drop a Remembrance . Rather, he drops the Heart of Bayle , which functions similarly, with some differences. The Heart of Bayle cannot be redeemed for an item by meeting with Finger Reader Enia . Rather, it must be redeemed at the Grand Altar of Dragon Communion . The Heart cannot be consumed to receive Runes, nor duplicated.

title: Bayle the Dread
text: Bayle's name is a corruption of "bale," which refers to a destructive and evil force. Bayle's name and moniker "The Dread" may also be a reference to the dragon Balerion , The Black Dread from author and the base game's co-writer George R. R. Martin's fantasy novel series, A Song of Ice and Fire .

title: Beastman of Farum Azula
text: Main article : Azula Beastmen The Azula beastmen are anthropomorphic, bestial creatures who reside in Crumbling Farum Azula . They fight using heavy, curved weapons forged from dull iron [ 1 ] , and some use shields made from earthenware jars, which are created to make shields. [ 2 ] The crumbling ruins of Farum Azula are said to be the remains of a giant mausoleum enshrining an Ancient Dragon , and certain Beastmen, who wield weapons clad in lightning, are chosen to guard it. [ 3 ]

Patches of their flesh are missing, exposing parts of their bones. They can be found wielding curved swords, jar-shields, or sickle-like throwing blades. Grey-haired Beastmen wield Clawmark Seals , which they use to cast lightning incantations. Some beastmen wield weapons imbued with red lightning, which deal lightning damage. title: Daggers
text: Daggers are a type of melee armament in Elden Ring .

title: Daggers
text: Daggers generally have very short reach and low damage against both health and poise, but compensates for this with their fast attack speed and sometimes higher than average damage on Critical Attacks , making several of them (particularly the Dagger and especially the Misericorde ) powerful tools for Backstabs and Ripostes . Their fast attack-speed and high number of hits-per-second makes them good at building up Status Effects like Poison and Blood Loss . As they have low weight, they also make excellent catalysts for certain Ashes of War and Skills , providing a stat-efficient means of casting status buffs like Golden Vow or Shared Order with no need for high investment in certain attributes, while specific daggers such as Reduvia and the Black Knife have exceptionally strong ranged Skills that only they posses ( Reduvia Blood Blade and Blade of Death respectively).

title: Daggers
text: The moveset for Daggers consists mostly of rapid slashes, with the exception of one- and two-handed rolling and heavy running attacks, two-handed heavy attacks, and dual wield backstep attacks, which are all thrusts. Some daggers have different standing heavy attacks: Glintstone Kris , Main-gauche , Miséricorde , and Scorpion's Stinger have thrusts, and Black Knife , Blade of Calling , Erdsteel Dagger , and Fire Knight's Shortsword have a quick, double-hit slash. The dual-wield moveset is notably quite quick and both the backstep attack and the jumping attack are excellent due to their reach and speed respectively. The first attack in the dual-wield attack chain is surprisingly slow due to being composed of 3 separate hits, making it somewhat difficult to use since Daggers won't stagger most foes.

title: Black Knife Assassin
text: Scions of the Eternal Cities , the Black Knife Assassins were a group of women with close ties to Queen Marika the Eternal . [ 1 ] Led by Alecto, Black Knife Ringleader , they were coconspirators of Ranni the Witch , who robbed the Rune of Death from Maliketh, the Black Blade . [ 2 ] She imbued it into Blades of Calling , fashioning a set of god -slaying Black Knives . [ 3 ] [ 4 ] The Assassins carried out the Night of Black Knives , carving Cursemarks of Death into Ranni and Godwyn the Golden . [ 5 ] They wore silent, silver scale armor and used Concealing Veils to mask their presence, rendering them effectively invisible. [ 6 ] [ 7 ] [ 8 ] Presently, they are scattered throughout the Lands Between , and can be found in many different regions .

title: Comet Azur
text: Azur's Glintstone Crown boosts attack power and FP cost by 15%. Azur's Glintstone Staff reduces casting time and increases FP costs by 20%. Cerulean Hidden Tear removes FP costs for 15 seconds. title: Comet Azur

Most bosses and enemies will dodge when the spell is initially cast, not when the beam is released.

title: Black Blade
text: | For the character, seek Maliketh, the Black Blade . For the weapon, seek Maliketh's Black Blade . Black Blade is an incantation spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the Erdtree Incantations . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effect 4 Notes 5 Trivia 6 See also

title: Black Blade
text: Power gleaned from the remembrance of Maliketh . Caster creates an illusory black blade , then leaps forward to deliver a spinning slash that emits a wave of light. This can be followed up with one additional attack. This blade was once imbued with Destined Death . In addition to dealing damage, it reduces foes' maximum HP and continues to sap their current HP for a very short time.

title: Black Blade
text: Trade the Remembrance of the Black Blade , dropped by Maliketh, the Black Blade , with Finger Reader Enia . Alternatively, use one of the Wandering Mausoleums to duplicate one (can only obtain one per playthrough using either method).

title: Black Blade
text: Summons a red, spectral version of Maliketh's Black Blade , then jumps and spins forwards with the blade to hit for Holy Attack Power equal to 225% of Incantation Scaling while also sending out a wave with Holy Attack Power equal to 162% of Incantation Scaling. If the incantation is used again immediately afterwards, the second sword's Attack Power is increased to 232% of Incantation Scaling while the beam's Attack Power is increased to 169% of Incantation Scaling for the second cast.

title: Black Blade
text: Costs 44 points of Stamina to perform, with the follow-up costing the same amount of Stamina for a total of 88 points of Stamina if both parts are used. The sword part deals 9 Stance damage and the beam part deals 4 Stance damage. Both the sword itself and the wave it sends out can hit the same target at melee range, meaning that the first cast effectively can have Attack Power roughly equal to 387% of Incantation Scaling while the follow-up cast can have Attack Power equal to 401% of Incantation Scaling. The beam attack applies two debuffs: the first deals damage equal to 0.1% of the target's maximum HP + 1 every 0.1 seconds for 2 seconds (total damage equal to 2% of target's maximum HP + 20) while the second debuff lowers the target's maximum HP by 10% for 15 seconds, signified by a part of their health bar being blocked out with grey.

title: Black Blade
text: The debuff that lowers maximum HP doesn't stack with the Maliketh's Black Blade's skill Destined Death similar debuff, but it does stack with the one inflicted by the Black Knife's skill Blade of Death .

title: Black Blade
text: Patch 1.08 fixed a bug that prevented the follow-up attack from occurring when the Black Sword Incantation was activated with a Sacred Seal in the left hand. Black Blade is incorrectly referred to as Black Sword in this patch note. Patch 1.07.1 the incantation Black Blade is currently missing its follow up attack when cast from the left hand. This issue will be corrected in a future update. The issue was fixed with Patch 1.08. Patch 1.07 reduced stamina consumption, improved turning performance and shortened recovery time, the sword and the wave attack have been changed so that they hit at the same time. Attack power, stamina attack power, and poise damage of each part have been decreased, decreased the number of times the wave part hits large enemies. Prior to this only either the sword attack or the beam attack could hit an enemy.

title: Black Blade
text: The Attack Power of the first sword and beam was decreased from 290% of Incantation Scaling and 252% of Incantation Scaling to 225% and 162% of the same respectively, and the Attack Power of the follow-up sword and beam was decreased from 297% of Incantation Scaling and 260% of Incantation Scaling to 232% and 169% of the same respectively. The Stamina damage to guarding opponents for the first sword and beam was decreased from 190 and 150 to 145 and 115 respectively, and the same for the follow-up sword and beam was decreased from 220 and 150 to 160 and 125 respectively. The Stamina damage to guarding players was split from the PvE values and increased at the same time, going to 181 and 143 for the first sword and beam, and to 200 and 156 for the follow-up sword and beam. The Stance damage of sword attacks and beam attacks were decreased from 10 and 7 to 9 and 4 respectively.

title: Black Blade
text: The Poise damage of sword attacks and beam attacks meanwhile was actually increased, not decreased, going from 100 and 70 to 243 and 108 respectively. Patch 1.04 decreased Stamina consumption and decreased recovery time.

title: Crystal Barrage
text: POWER (uncharged): 13.5% SS per bolt, 6 shots total (81% full barrage) POWER (charged): 13.5% SS per bolt, up to 23 shots total (310.5% at 48 FP full barrage) An astoundingly poor spell in PvE, without charging it has a terribly high cost for utterly miserable damage that's barely over half that of a single Glintstone Pebble , and if waiting to charge it this is extremely slow; simply spamming other spells will heavily outdamage it over time. Charging the attack does not increase the damage, it merely extends it for more shots at an additional 2 FP per shot, up to 17 extra bolts total. The one notable exception would be in Invasion situations, both players and NPC Bloody Fingers. Such highly evasive opponents would find it rather difficult to evade the entire barrage of a fully charged shot.

text: Create a small ball of Blackflame to throw, hitting with Fire Attack Power equal to 244% of Incantation Scaling and inflicting a Blackflame effect that deals damage equal to 2% of target's maximum HP + 20, over the course of 2 seconds. Charging the spell increases the Attack Power to 300% of Incantation Scaling.

title: Black Flame
text: Costs 25 points of Stamina to cast. Charging the spell increases the cost to 33 points. Deals 9.1 Stance damage on hit. Charging the spell increases the Stance damage to 11.83. Attack Power is boosted by 15% by the Godfrey Icon if charged. Since charging the spell already increases Attack Power by 23%, wearing this talisman increases the Attack Power by a total of 41%. Attack Power is boosted by 15% per Godslayer's Seal held in an active hand (2 max). An very strong yet simple spell due to a combination of fast casting speed and percentage-based damage. Since it deals at least 2% of a target's health per cast it can kill anything damageable in 50 casts, though most often much quicker due to the Fire damage it also deals.

title: Black Flame
text: All bosses in Shadow of the Erdtree are resistant to the percentage-based damage of Blackflame , only taking damage equal to 1% of target's maximum HP + 10 over 2 seconds. Messmer the Impaler , Bayle the Dread , and the Promised Consort are even more resistant to the effect, only taking damage equal to 0.7% of target's maximum HP + 7.

title: Black Flame
text: 1.07 : Increased stamina attack power against guarded enemies. Stamina Attack Power against guarding enemies was increased from 100 to 150 for the uncharged version, and from 160 to 210 for the charged version. Against players the Stamina Attack Power was instead increased to 187 for the uncharged version and to 262 for the charged version. 1.04 : Increase damage and the spell can break enemy’s guard more easily. Attack Power was increased from 214% of Incantation Scaling for the uncharged version to 244% of the same, and from 248% of Incantation Scaling for the charged version to 300% of the same. Stamina Attack Power against guarding enemies was increased from 91 to 100 for the uncharged version, and from 118 to 160 for the charged version. Patch 1.03.3 seems to have included an undocumented change which increased the FP cost of this incantation from 15 to 18.
