title: Black Flame Blade
text: A black flame incantation of the Godskin Apostles . Engulfs Armament held in the right hand with black flame . This incantation can be cast without delay after performing another action. Black flame is weighty. After dealing damage, it continues to sap foes' HP for a very short time.

title: Black Flame Blade
text: Purchase: Roundtable Hold / Liurnia of the Lakes / Altus Plateau / Leyndell, Royal Capital / Mountaintops of the Giants Sold for 6,000 Runes by Brother Corhyn or Miriel, Pastor of Vows after giving either of them the Godskin Prayerbook . Should the prayerbook be given to one of them and then have that instructor die before purchasing this Incantation , the player can still purchase it from the Twin Maiden Husks for the same price after giving them the respective tutor's Bell Bearing . WARNING: Should both Corhyn and Miriel die before giving either of them the prayerbook, this incantation becomes unavailable for that character until a new Journey is started.

title: Black Flame Blade
text: Quickly sheathe the right-hand weapon with Blackflame , granting Fire Attack Power equal to 85% of the seal's Faith -based Incantation Scaling and inflicting an effect that deals damage equal to 2% of target's maximum HP + 20, over the course of 2 seconds. Lasts for 7 seconds and counts as a Weapon Buff .

title: Black Flame Blade
text: Costs 10 points of Stamina to cast. Despite being one of the Godslayer Incantations , this spell, like other spell-based weapon buffs, does not get bonus damage from its associated seal (the Godslayer's Seal for this one). Can have its extremely short duration extended by 30% by the Old Lord's Talisman , to 9.1 seconds. The Blackflame effect is also applied to target's hit by Skills of the buffed weapon, even if those skills don't involve the weapon, such as with Skill: Thunderbolt or Skill: Flame of the Redmanes . Casts considerably quicker if used immediately after a roll, jump, or attack, or when sprinting, letting the user get more out of its short duration. The additional damage from the Blackflame effect will at most be 9% of target's maximum HP + 90 (11.1% + 110 if the Old Lord's Talisman is used).

title: Black Flame Blade
text: Practically though, the bonus damage will probably be around 5% + 50, presuming the user is able to keep on the offense. It's important to note that the duration of Blackflame effects doesn't stack, instead the duration is reset if another hit is landed during an ongoing burn. Due to the extremely short duration of this buff, it's important to keep on the offensive if possible after casting the spell. Should ideally be cast just before an opening in a boss's attacks happens. Outmatched in added Fire Attack Power (i.e. not counting the added Blackflame effect) by Drawstring Fire Grease below 170 Incantation Scaling and by Drawstring Messmerfire Grease below 208 Incantation Scaling. Note that both of these consumable items last considerably longer than Black Flame Blade, even if the Old Lord's Talisman is equipped.

title: Black Flame Blade
text: More specifically, Drawstring Fire Grease lasts for 10 seconds and Drawstring Messmerfire Grease lasts for 25 seconds. Can be used with a quick weapon such as the Dagger , even if it's unupgraded, to deal heavy damage to high-health bosses. All bosses in Shadow of the Erdtree are resistant to the percentage-based damage of Blackflame , only taking damage equal to 1% of target's maximum HP + 10 over 2 seconds. Messmer the Impaler , Bayle the Dread , and the Promised Consort are even more resistant to the effect, only taking damage equal to 0.7% of target's maximum HP + 7.

title: Black Flame Blade
text: Uses the same casting animation as Drawstring Greases, and also share their very short duration (though Black Flame Blade's duration is shorter than even theirs).

title: Bloodhound Knight
text: Bloodhound Knights are trained as hunters and are known to be unshakable trackers. Without language, they choose their own master whom they will stay loyal to for the rest of their life. [ 1 ] They wield a Bloodhound's Fang alongside a Bloodhound Claw that allows them to slip past an enemy's guard. They use the Ash of War: Bloodhound's Step to either move out of harm's way or to close the gap between them and their opponent quickly. Lesser variants can be found as non-respawning enemies guarding corpses in the overworld.

title: Black Flame Ritual
text: Superior black flame incantation of the Godskin Apostles . Summons a circle of black flame pillars around the caster. Charging increases the size of the circle. The Gloam-Eyed Queen led the apostles. It is said that she was an Empyrean chosen by the Fingers .

title: Black Flame Ritual
text: Guaranteed Drop: Mountaintops of the Giants Black Flame Ritual is received upon defeating the Spiritcaller Snail boss in Spiritcaller Cave on the Mountaintops of the Giants . This Snail summons the spirits of a Godskin Apostle and a Godskin Noble .

title: Black Flame Ritual
text: Summon a circle of Blackflame around the caster for about 4 seconds, hitting with Fire Attack Power equal to 106% of Incantation Scaling and inflicting an effect that deals damage equal to 2% of target's maximum HP + 20, over the course of 2 seconds, if an enemy touches them. Charging the spell increases the Attack Power to 109% of Incantation Scaling and creates the circle a bit further away from the caster.

title: Black Flame Ritual
text: Costs 39 points of Stamina to cast. Charging the spell increases the cost to 51 points. Deals 5 Stance damage per hit. Charging the spell increases the Stance damage to 6.5 per hit. Attack Power is boosted by 15% by the Godfrey Icon if charged. Since charging the spell already increases Attack Power by 3%, wearing this talisman increases the Attack Power by a total of 18%. Attack Power is boosted by 15% per Godslayer's Seal held in an active hand (2 max). The ring can hit an enemy up to 4 times, effectively granting it Attack Power equal to 424% of Incantation Scaling (436% if charged), not accounting for flat defence, and dealing additional damage equal to 5% of target's maximum HP + 20 (since hitting with a new Blackflame effect will just reset the duration of the debuff).

title: Black Flame Ritual
text: This makes the incantation surprisingly usable for attacking, at least against large opponents such as Trolls , since enemies can be tricked into standing inside of the flames. An older instance of Black Flame Ritual isn't removed when a new is cast, though their damage won't stack as efficiently against opponents compared to when their casts are more separated. Significantly harder to use against other players, being primarily useful for forcing them to stay away from the caster or to obscure the caster's other attacks. Be wary of opponent's jumping over the flames or simply running through them, as well as the fact that the charged version's pillars being further out means it's possible to stand close to the caster without being damaged.

title: Black Flame Ritual
text: All bosses in Shadow of the Erdtree are resistant to the percentage-based damage of Blackflame , only taking damage equal to 1% of target's maximum HP + 10 over 2 seconds. Messmer the Impaler , Bayle the Dread , and the Promised Consort are even more resistant to the effect, only taking damage equal to 0.7% of target's maximum HP + 7.

text: Black Flame's Protection is an incantation spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the Godslayer Incantations . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effects 4 Notes 4.1 Strategies 5 See also

title: Black Flame's Protection
text: A black flame incantation of the Godskin Apostles . Summons black fire within, increasing physical damage negation. However, sacred flasks and other such forms of HP restoration are impaired. The Apostles were all embraced by the Gloam-Eyed Queen , and the black flame was their armor within.

title: Black Flame's Protection
text: Purchase: Roundtable Hold Acquired from Sir Gideon Ofnir, the All-Knowing after selecting the "about the secret medallion" dialogue option. This option will only appear after the player has obtained both the Haligtree Secret Medallion (Right) and Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left) . Can be purchased from the Twin Maiden Husks for 2,500 Runes should the player not talk to Gideon about both medallions before unleashing the Rune of Death .

title: Black Flame's Protection
text: Grants a Body Buff that decreases Physical damage taken by 35% (15% in PvP) while also decreasing all healing received by 20% for 70 seconds.

title: Black Flame's Protection
text: Costs 30 FP per cast. Costs 13 Stamina per cast. Requires 1 Memory Slot to be equipped. This spell reduces Physical damage taken rather than directly increasing damage negation .

title: Black Flame's Protection
text: The total damage reduction can be expressed by the following formula, where PhysicalFortification represents the effect magnitude: T o t a l P h y s i c a l R e d u c t i o n = 100 ( 1 − ( 1 − P h y s i c a l D a m a g e N e g a t i o n × 0.01 ) × ( 1 − P h y s i c a l F o r t i f i c a t i o n ) ) {\displaystyle TotalPhysicalReduction=100(1-(1-{PhysicalDamageNegation}\times 0.01)\times (1-{PhysicalFortification}))} For example, if physical damage negation is 25 at the time of casting, the total reduction against PvE enemies would be 51.25%: P h y s i c a l M a g i c R e d u c t i o n = 100 ( 1 − ( 1 − 25 × 0.01 ) × ( 1 − 0.35 ) ) = 51.25 % {\displaystyle PhysicalMagicReduction=100(1-(1-25\times 0.01)\times (1-0.35))=51.25\%} Against other players, the total reduction would be 36.25%: T o t a l P h y s i c a l R e d u c t i o n = 100 ( 1 − ( 1 − 25 × 0.01 ) × ( 1 − 0.15 ) ) = 36.25 % {\displaystyle TotalPhysicalReduction=100(1-(1-25\times 0.01)\times (1-0.15))=36.25\%}

title: Black Flame's Protection
text: The reduction in healing effective lowers the effectiveness of this spell to only lowering Physical damage taken by 18.75% (and increasing the effective damage taken in PvP by 6.25%), assuming that healing will be used and not accounting for it helping the player survive hits that otherwise would have killed them. Effectively still has its full PvP strength of 15% in no-healing scenarios such as semi-formal duels in the open world or via a Duelist's Furled Finger , and the Duel Arena Combat mode available through the Royal Colosseum in Leyndell, Royal Capital or via Roundtable Hold . The effectiveness of this spell diminishes as physical damage negation increases, offering less benefit when stacked with multiple physical negation sources.

title: Black Flame's Protection
text: This incantation is valuable whenever available since the vast majority of areas will have most of the damage inflicted by enemies as various types of Physical damage. The biggest exception is Liurnia of the Lakes , where Magic damage shares the spotlight. Black Flame's Protection offers greater physical protection than Boiled Crab (35% vs. 20%) and has a slightly longer duration (70 vs. 60 seconds). This spell requires 30 Faith and 1 Memory Slot to be equipped and 30 FP to cast, while the Crab only requires 600 Runes per piece bought. The Boiled Crab is preferable for PvP encounters, as Black Flame's Protection's effect is reduced to 15%. Though Boiled Crab (and the slightly weaker Boiled Prawn ) are potentially accessible much earlier, it's possible to not gain access to them should Blackguard Big Boggart be killed before any of either has been bought from him.

title: Black Flame's Protection
text: The reduction in healing for the Flask of Crimson Tears specifically can be countered with either the Crimson Seed Talisman or Crimson Seed Talisman +1 , setting healing effectiveness to 96% and 104% respectively. Note that this only helps with the flask, as the Heal spells are not affected by either talisman but is affected by the healing reduction.

Shockwaves from slams fan out in front; positioning behind him mitigates most follow-ups.

title: Boltdrake Talisman +3
text: It can be found at Shadow Keep . From the Storehouse: First Floor site of grace , take the stairs up to the second floor and find a dead-end with several shade protectors. Jump over the railing to reach a room with the talisman, then use the lever to move the bookcase and re-emerge near the site of grace. title: Borealis the Freezing Fog
text: Main article : Dragon Borealis the Freezing Fog was one of the ice dragons, who were lords of the Mountaintops before the Fire Giants chased them from the peak. [ 1 ] The dragon Borealis patrols the frozen expanse of the Freezing Lake in the northeastern Mountaintops of the Giants. [ 1 ] He emerges from a thick white fog, which he exhales clouds that inflict Frostbite .

title: Borealis the Freezing Fog

Location: Mountaintops of the Giants ( Freezing Lake ), HP: 11,268, Runes: 100,000, Drops: Dragon Heart

title: Borealis the Freezing Fog
text: Instead of flames, Borealis exhales Dragonice and Borealis's Mist . Borealis inflicts Frostbite with his breath attacks and roars. His attacks include: Frost Breath: Borealis exhales a cone of freezing fog, which lingers and applies Frostbite . Frost Stomp: Stomps on the ground, creating a shockwave of ice that deals AoE damage and builds Frostbite . Frost Wing Sweep: Swings his massive wings, releasing icy projectiles that track players in the area. Roar: Releases a deafening roar that briefly staggers players and applies frost buildup.

title: Borealis the Freezing Fog
text: Borealis the Freezing Fog counts as a Dragon -type enemy and is vulnerable to anti-dragon effects. Furled Fingers and Spirit Ashes are allowed in this encounter. Defeating Borealis the Freezing Fog will unlock Borealis's Mist , which can be obtained at the altar in the Cathedral of Dragon Communion in Caelid for two Dragon Hearts .

Contents 1 Background 2 Chacteristics 3 Resistances 4 Locations 5 Bosses 6 Notes 7 Gallery 8 See Also 8.1 External Links 9 References

title: Cleanrot Knight
text: The Cleanrot Knights were fierce warriors who remained devoted to Malenia, Blade of Miquella , even as her scarlet rot consumed their bodies. [ 1 ] Their winged helms and sword insignias embodied the unyielding resolve of their Empyrean master. [ 2 ] They stood in arms with Miquella 's followers within Elphael, Brace of the Haligtree . They fought under Malenia's command throughout her undefeated campaign in the Shattering , [ 1 ] which culminated in the fateful clash against General Radahn 's Redmane army in Caelid . A majority of the knights fell at the Battle of Aeonia , their spirits locked in eternal combat in the War-Dead Catacombs . The few survivors wandered the resulting Swamp of Aeonia , while others traced the footsteps of Cleanrot Knight Finlay , who bore the sleeping Malenia all the way back to the Haligtree , [ 3 ] passing through the Shaded Castle along the way.

title: Cleanrot Knight
text: All Cleanrot Knights wear golden armor and wield a Cleanrot Knight's Sword as a secondary weapon to complement their polearms. [ 4 ] This allows them to land multiple attacks in quick succession, triggering the Talisman's blessing to strengthen them even further. Most Cleanrot Knights wield a Cleanrot Spear , which bears a small shield on its shaft. They will imbue their spear with gold before performing their more deadly attacks, such as grabbing their enemy with the spear tip and infecting them with Scarlet Rot, and summoning forth a golden palisade from the ground. [ 5 ] They can also brace their spear with their sword to absorb an incoming blow and quickly retaliate with their own. Cleanrot knight commanders wield the Halo Scythe . [ 6 ] They prefer ranged combat, repeatedly firing rings of Miquella's light from a distance. [ 7 ] In close quarters, they may vomit Scarlet Rot.

title: Cleanrot Knight
text: Cleanrot Knights are resistant to Slash and Holy damage, taking 35% and 40% less damage from these types. They receive flat damage from Pierce and Lightning damage, but take 20% extra damage from Fire attacks; however, the scythe-wielding variant can nullify this vulnerability by casting an incantation . title: Commander Gaius
text: Commander Gaius was a noted warrior and military strategist who rode a giant boar as his steed. Gaius studied gravitational magic from an Alabaster Lord . [ 1 ] He was a fellow student of Radahn , a future demigod . He also befriended Messmer the Impaler , as both had in common that they were cursed from birth. The pair were as elder brothers to Radahn, with whom Gaius had formed a friendly rivalry. [ 2 ] [ 3 ] When Messmer embarked on the purge of the Land of Shadow on Queen Marika the Eternal 's orders, Gaius would join him and serve as the commander of his soldiers. He was never without his boar. [ 4 ] Even when Marika abandoned Messmer, Gaius remained steadfast, guarding the backdoor of Shadow Keep . It was here that he would meet his end at the hands of a Tarnished champion .

title: Commander Gaius
text: Commander Gaius is an albinauric without legs who rides a heavily armored, great-tusked boar. Gaius wears black iron armor patterned to represent fallen seeds and wields the Sword Lance . [ 1 ] He was cursed by his Albinauric heritage and as such was doomed to lose the use of his legs. [ 5 ]

title: Commander Gaius
text: Commander Gaius will charge with his boar to try to run you over. This can be dodge by jumping over with torrent or dodging at a very exact moment. He also uses gravity magic to attack enemies that are too far away.

title: Commander Gaius

title: Borealis's Mist
text: Borealis's Mist is an incantation spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the Dragon Communion Incantations . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effects 4 Notes 5 Trivia 6 See also

title: Borealis's Mist
text: Superior incantation of Dragon Communion . Channels the power of Borealis the Freezing Fog . Transforms caster into a dragon to spew icy breath from above. Charging extends duration. This incantation can be cast while jumping. The ice dragons were once lords of the mountaintops long ago, until they were defeated by the Fire Giants and chased from the peak .

title: Borealis's Mist
text: Dragon Communion: Caelid / Jagged Peak Acquired from the altar at the Cathedral of Dragon Communion in Caelid and the Grand Altar of Dragon Communion on the Jagged Peak for 2 Dragon Hearts after defeating Borealis the Freezing Fog on the Mountaintops of the Giants .

title: Borealis's Mist
text: The caster floats up into the air and summons a dragon head, before breathing out a wave of frozen mist, dealing Magic damage equal to 158% of Incantation Scaling and inflicting 110 Frostbite build-up up to 3 times (i.e. 474% of Incantation Scaling and 330 Frostbite build-up cumulatively) if the entire wave hits. Continue channeling the spell to let loose a second breath, also hitting up to 3 times.

title: Borealis's Mist
text: Costs 60 points of Stamina to cast, and another 60 points upon using the follow-up breath. Damage is boosted by 10% by the Roar Medallion . Damage is boosted by 15% per Dragon Communion Seal held in an active hand (2 max). Damage is boosted by 20% when the Rock Heart is used. This spell can be cast midair but cannot be cast while mounted, unlike Dragonice . Of the three Frostbite-causing Dragon Breaths (this spell, Dragonice , and Ghostflame Breath ), this is the most expensive one, but it effectively combines the high Frostbite build-up of Dragonice with the higher Magic damage of Ghostflame Breath. Compared to Dragonice , this spell deals 17% more damage but also consumes 38% more FP . Compared to Ghostflame Breath , this spell deals 6% less damage and consumes 17% more FP but causes 100% more Frostbite build-up.

title: Borealis's Mist
text: Casting this spell until it stops costs 168 FP: 2*48 FP from the two breaths and 12*6 FP from keeping the spell running. Only 91 FP is actually needed for the entire routine. This is just enough to start the second breath, and thereafter the game does not care that no FP remains as it will continue casting regardless. This is the second weakest "named" dragon breath attack, dealing 16% less damage than Smarag's Glintstone Breath and 7% less damage than Agheel's Flame (the latter is only if an enemy's Fire Damage Negation and Magic Damage Negation is equal), and deals only 2% more damage than Ekzykes's Decay . However, the Frostbite build-up of the spell can even the field against non-immune enemies as it deals a significant amount of percentage-based damage and lowers their Damage Negation against further attacks. Deals 20% less damage against other players.

title: Borealis's Mist
text: Patch 1.07 the power of the following incantations in PvP has been decreased. The multiplier for damage against other players was lowered from 100% to 80%.

title: Commander Niall
text: Commander Niall is an optional boss in Elden Ring . Defeating him will unlock the Commander Niall achievement . Contents 1 Overview 2 Moveset 3 Notes 4 Trivia 5 Gallery 6 See Also 7 References

title: Commander Niall
text: Commander Niall, a veteran general of Castle Sol , offered his prosthesis in exchange for the lives of imprisoned knights , forming an army of no nation. [ 1 ] A lone survivor, Niall commands the spirits of deceased knights to defend his master, who has long passed. [ 2 ] Niall guards a chest containing the Haligtree Secret Medallion (Left) , one-half of the Medallion required to gain access to the Consecrated Snowfield , the land of Miquella's Haligtree .

title: Commander Niall
text: At the beginning of the fight, Niall summons the spirits of two Banished Knights , one with a sword and shield, and the other wielding dual swords. Niall will be relatively passive while the knights are alive, instead buffing them until they have both been defeated or his health has been reduced to half. Both Banished Knights will inflict Frostbite with their attacks. Niall himself uses a mixture of lightning and frost -based attacks. He can imbue his prosthesis with lightning and perform kicks and stomps, and he can use his halberd to generate frost.

title: Commander Niall
text: Internal name: OldGeneral (ID: 3050) The Japanese description for Veteran's Prosthesis states Niall offered his leg, rather than his prosthetic, in exchange for his knights.

title: Burn, O Flame!
text: Burn, O Flame! is an incantation spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the Giants' Flame incantations . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effects 4 Notes 4.1 Boosting 4.2 Strategies 5 Trivia 6 See also

title: Burn, O Flame!
text: One of the incantations that draws directly from the power of the Fire Giants . Raises a series of flame pillars around the caster. Charging further increases the number of flame pillars. The Fire Giants borrowed from the power of a fell god , and still they were defeated . Yet their failure released them from their solitary curse: to serve as keepers of the Flame for eternity.

title: Burn, O Flame!
text: Trade the Remembrance of the Fire Giant , gained by defeating the Fire Giant , with Finger Reader Enia . Alternatively, use one of the Wandering Mausoleums to duplicate one (can only obtain one per playthrough using either method).

title: Burn, O Flame!
text: Crouch down to raise a series of pillars of flame around the caster, hitting with Fire Attack Power equal to 288% of Incantation Scaling . Charging increases the number of flame pillars. [citation needed]

title: Burn, O Flame!
text: Costs 26 FP per cast. Costs 36 Stamina per cast. Requires 1 Memory Slot . Deals 9.25 Stance damage per pillar that hits. Charging the spell only slightly increases the casting time.

title: Burn, O Flame!
text: Fire Scorpion Charm increases Fire Attack Power by 12% (8% in PvP). Flame, Grant Me Strength increases Fire Attack Power by 20% (15% in PvP) for 30 seconds as a Body Buff . Flame-Shrouding Cracked Tear increases Fire Attack Power by 20% (15% in PvP) for 3 minutes. Giant's Seal increases Attack Power by 20% when held in an active hand, and by 44% when wielded in each hand. Attack Power is boosted by 15% by the Godfrey Icon if charged.

title: Burn, O Flame!
text: This spell is quite devastating against large targets who may be struck by multiple pillars. Since the caster kneels down when casting they are harder to hit for some chest- or head-height attacks. Smaller, humanoid enemies ( Melenia , Any Footsoldier , etc...) will be thrown into the air after being hit by a pillar.

title: Burn, O Flame!
text: This spell draws upon the same power as other Giants' Flame Incantations . Patch 1.07 : Reduced FP consumption. Shortened the time it takes for a flame pillar to be generated. FP cost was reduced from 30 to 26. Patch 1.04 : Increased cast speed and decreased recovery time.

title: Heavy Thrusting Swords
text: Heavy Thrusting Swords are a type of melee armament in Elden Ring .

title: Heavy Thrusting Swords
text: They have moderately quick attack speed and reasonable range as well as more damage against health , poise and have a relatively subtle telegraphs at the start of most of their attack animations.  However they are overall slower and have a higher stamina cost compared to smaller Thrusting Swords Similar to Curved Swords and Thrusting Swords , their heavy attack charges can be canceled in favor of a quick feint-attack should the player press the Roll button while charging. They can also attack while guarding at the same time similar to smaller to their smaller brethren and Spears . Their two-handed moveset is generally very similar to their one-handed one, increasing overall damage to health and poise.

title: Commander O'Neil
text: Commander O'Neil is a boss in Elden Ring . Contents 1 Overview 2 Moveset 3 Notes 4 Trivia 5 Gallery 6 See Also 7 References

title: Commander O'Neil
text: A veteran of a battle that took place in the Swamp of Aeonia in Caelid . He continued to brandish his flag in the rot-eaten battlefield even after his lord fled. [ 1 ] He wears the same armor and wields the same standard as Commander Niall , the leader of an army of no nation situated in Castle Sol . In battle, Commander O'Neil summons the spirits of Exile Soldiers . Initially, he will summon five regular soldiers, and will summon three large soldiers after his health has been reduced to half. His attacks inflict Scarlet Rot . O'Neil is in possession of the Unalloyed Gold Needle, which is required by Sage Gowry to stanch Millicent 's Rot. Polyanna, Adopted Daughter can be summoned for this fight.

title: Catch Flame
text: Catch Flame is an incantation spell in Elden Ring . It is one of the Flame incantations . Contents 1 Description 2 Acquisition 3 Effects 4 Notes 4.1 Boosting 4.2 Strategies 5 Trivia 6 See also 7 References

title: Catch Flame
text: Incantation originating from a sinister prophecy . Momentarily sparks flame from the caster's hand. This incantation can be cast without delay after performing another action. The flame of ruin is anathema to the Erdtree . But prophets sometimes glimpse it within the faith all the same. Sadly when this occurs their sole reward is banishment.

title: Catch Flame
text: Purchase: Roundtable Hold / Altus Plateau / Leyndell, Royal Capital / Mountaintops of the Giants Sold for 600 Runes by Brother Corhyn . Should Corhyn die before purchasing this incantation, then the player can still purchase it from the Twin Maiden Husks for the same price, after giving them Corhyn's Bell Bearing . Starting equipment for the Prophet Origin .

title: Catch Flame
text: Conjure a small burst of flames from the hand, that has Fire Attack Power equal to 225% of Incantation Scaling .

title: Catch Flame
text: Costs 10 FP per cast. Costs 17 Stamina per cast. Requires 1 Memory Slot . Deals 7 Stance damage. Can be chain-cast continuously, provided the player has sufficient amounts of FP. Casting this incantation directly after a roll, jump, or backstep makes the casting time slightly shorter, making it much more dynamic than many other, slower spells. Moves the caster slightly forward with each use. Despite using the Giants' Flame , this spell does not generate a spell sigil when cast, making it ineligible for a boost from the Giant's Seal .

title: Catch Flame
text: Fire Scorpion Charm increases Fire Attack Power by 12% (8% in PvP). Flame, Grant Me Strength increases Fire Attack Power by 20% (15% in PvP) for 30 seconds as a Body Buff . Flame-Shrouding Cracked Tear increases Fire Attack Power by 20% (15% in PvP) for 3 minutes.

title: Catch Flame
text: With its rapid casting time, Catch Flame excels as a quick, close-range option for dispatching enemies. However, its small area of effect and short range make it less reliable against agile or fast-moving targets. A potent damage-dealing spell throughout the game thanks to its more than decent damage and extremely quick casting time, and available on a lot of Incantations build with only a minimal amount of Faith . It is especially potent as an early-game tool for players who can manage the low FP cost compared to their meager FP pool. Highly efficient as a melee spell for pure Faith builds. Casting Catch Flame immediately before Magma Breath significantly reduces the cast time of the latter, enabling quicker execution in combat.

title: Catch Flame
text: When chained into Magma Breath mid-air, Catch Flame modifies the casting animation into a faster, more fluid spray, allowing players to seamlessly chain another Magma Breath cast.

title: Catch Flame
text: Like Fire's Deadly Sin and Flame Sling , this incantation does not produce a spell sigil when cast, indicating that it technically doesn't belong to the Giants' Flame subtype and isn't boosted by the Giant's Seal . Brother Corhyn was one of the prophets exiled for glimpsing the forbidden flame of ruin, a transgression that led to banishment. [ 1 ] Catch Flame closely resembles the Combustion spell from the Dark Souls series.
